summary: The diff implements all three requirements: REQ-1 (backend RI-format unique member ID generation), REQ-2 (updated RegisterPage with validated form and success display), and REQ-3 (AdminPanel member ID column and React Query cache invalidation). Most acceptance criteria are met. One minor concern is the React Query cache invalidation after registration — the diff summary mentions it but does not show explicit invalidation code in useQueries.ts. However, the frontend-file-summaries.txt describes useRegisterMember as using React Query mutations, which typically include invalidation. Overall coverage is strong.

missing_requirements.requirement: The admin panel React Query cache is invalidated after a new member is registered so the list reflects the latest data.
missing_requirements.reason: The diff does not show the useQueries.ts content in detail (only a summary description), so there is no direct evidence that the useRegisterMember mutation explicitly calls queryClient.invalidateQueries for the members list after a successful registration. The file summary describes the hook but does not confirm cache invalidation logic.

hallucinated_features.description: A migration module (backend/migration.mo) was added to migrate existing member records to include the new memberIdStr field.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request only asked to update registration logic to generate and store RI-format IDs. It did not request a migration module for existing data. While this is a reasonable implementation choice, it was not part of the specified requirements.
